name: Kurinoye File So Smorchkani (Stuffed Chicken B
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Chicken Stock or Canned Chicken Broth
1 large boneless chicken breast, cut into halves
Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
1/2 cup heavy cream or whipping cream
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1/2 cup of chicken stock to a boil and pour over the morels in a heatproof bowl. Soak for 1 hour, then drain (reserve the liquid) and chop fine.
2. Strain the soaking liquid through a coffee filter and set aside.
3. Pound each chicken breast half between two sheets of waxed paper with the flat side of a meat pounder until thin.
4. In a small bowl, combine ground chicken, beaten egg, 1 tablespoon parsley, dill, and 2 tablespoons chopped morels. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Place half of the filling along the long side of each breast half, tuck in the ends, and roll up. Secure with a wooden toothpick.
6. Place the chicken rolls in a medium-sized saucepan and add the morel soaking liquid, remaining broth, wine, and remaining morels. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and partially cover, poaching the chicken until cooked through (20 minutes).
7. With a slotted spoon, remove the chicken to a heated platter.
8. Over medium-high heat, reduce the poaching liquid to 1/2 cup, stirring for 15 minutes. Add cream and cook for an additional 7 minutes.
9. Remove the pan from the heat and whisk butter and vermouth until well blended in the liquid. Stir in remaining parsley and season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. To serve, cut the rolls into 1/2-inch slices and spoon sauce over them.
